data.title: Refactor pre-commit-ticket-gate.sh to use ticket exists
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Refactor `pre-commit-ticket-gate.sh` to call `ticket exists` instead of performing direct filesystem checks (directory + CREATE event file presence). Preserve hot-path performance characteristics — the gate runs on every commit and must remain sub-10ms per lookup. Add to boundary hook allowlist as a thin wrapper.

**Why**: Moves the pre-commit ticket-existence check into the ticket CLI boundary while preserving the archive-agnostic semantics the gate relies on.

**Scope**:
- IN: pre-commit-ticket-gate.sh refactor to use `.claude/scripts/dso ticket exists` (via shim), performance verification that each invocation still completes well within the pre-commit budget, existing test suite passes, boundary-hook allowlist entry with ticket citation
- OUT: Changes to `ticket exists` itself (epic 2c28-e5b9 scope); any change to gate behavior (the gate's pass/fail semantics are preserved)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, pre-commit-ticket-gate.sh invokes `.claude/scripts/dso ticket exists` (via shim) and preserves the existing archive-agnostic existence-check semantics (archived tickets return exit 0)
  ← Satisfies: "pre-commit-ticket-gate.sh calls ticket exists"
- When this story is complete, pre-commit-ticket-gate.sh's existing test suite passes with no behavioral regression
  ← Satisfies: "Each refactored script passes its existing test suite with no behavioral regression"
- When this story is complete, pre-commit-ticket-gate.sh is added to the boundary hook allowlist with an inline comment citing epic e1b2-2653
- When this story is complete, performance measurement confirms the gate's total runtime on a representative commit remains within the pre-commit budget (no regression vs. direct filesystem check)
- When this story is complete, unit tests pass for all modified logic

## Considerations

- [Performance] The gate runs on every commit — verify that `ticket exists` maintains sub-10ms per-lookup characteristics (story 4c60-5e2e from epic 2c28-e5b9 owns the performance SLO)
- [Reliability] Archived-ticket semantics must be preserved — committed tickets that reference archived IDs must still pass the gate
- [Dependency] Depends on `ticket exists` from epic 2c28-e5b9 with archive-agnostic semantics (inter-epic)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
